articles.title: ‘She is truly a superhero’: Why a Belmont athlete is running Boston for his wife
articles.description:
"I’m running for her and so many people like her. To show them that there is always a finish line."

The post ‘She is truly a superhero’: Why a Belmont athlete is running Boston for his wife appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Belmont athlete Ben Flowers is running the Boston Marathon to honor his wife.
Flowers aims to inspire others facing challenges, emphasizing hope and perseverance.
The event highlights local resilience, fostering community support for those in need.

articles.title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support kids with cancer
articles.description:
Kathleen Labrie is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Massachusetts General Hospital Pediatric Cancer team.

The post She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support kids with cancer appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Kathleen Labrie will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise funds for pediatric cancer care.
Funds will support the Massachusetts General Hospital Pediatric Cancer team and local children in need.
The marathon fosters community support for families affected by childhood cancer in Boston.

articles.title: Noah Kahan announces premiere date for documentary coming to Netflix
articles.description:
The New England-grown singer-songwriter will offer a new perspective on his life and music in "Noah Kahan: Out of Body," a documentary set to release next month.

The post Noah Kahan announces premiere date for documentary coming to Netflix appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Noah Kahan's documentary 'Out of Body' premieres on Netflix next month.
The film provides insights into Kahan's life and music as a New England artist.
Local fans can expect a deeper connection to Kahan's experiences and inspirations.

articles.title: Jon Keller, longtime WBZ political analyst, returns to TV
articles.description:
Keller was laid off last fall but is returning to WBZ as a "special contributor" beginning this week.

The post Jon Keller, longtime WBZ political analyst, returns to TV appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Jon Keller returns to WBZ as a special contributor starting this week.
Keller was previously laid off last fall after many years at WBZ.
His return enhances local political analysis for Boston residents.

articles.title: ‘A relic of a bygone era’: Here’s why readers want Massachusetts to stop changing its clocks
articles.description:
Though most readers were in favor of ending the twice-a-year clock change, many differed on which time system the state should adopt permanently.

The post ‘A relic of a bygone era’: Here’s why readers want Massachusetts to stop changing its clocks appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Majority of readers support ending the biannual clock change in Massachusetts.
Debate exists on whether to adopt permanent Daylight Saving Time or Standard Time.
Changing the clock system could affect local businesses and daily routines for Boston residents.
